tests.name: init_sh_non_interactive_exits_zero
tests.details: init.sh --non-interactive completed with exit 0 against mobile/light/organizational/production/swift config. Fake-remote push soft-failed as expected (PR #18).

tests.name: context7_detection
tests.details: verify-install.sh from project dir reported Context7 MCP installed and configured (53 OK, 0 manual)

tests.name: framework_self_guard_active
tests.details: Running init.sh from $FRAMEWORK exited 1 with 'Refusing to operate inside the Solo Orchestrator framework repo' message (PR #18 U-N/U-O fix holds)

tests.name: each_phase1_step_completes
tests.details: All 5 PHASE1_STEPS (architecture_selected, threat_model_complete, data_model_defined, ui_scaffolding_done, bible_synthesized) completed sequentially with exit 0; final 'All steps complete for phase1_architecture' message

tests.name: phase1_to_phase2_gate_passes
tests.details: check-phase-gate.sh consistency-check exit 0 reporting 'Phase gates consistent' at phase 0; gate is read-only and does not advance current_phase

tests.name: phase_state_advances_to_2
tests.details: U-D still open: no script auto-advances .current_phase after gate. Had to manually `jq '.current_phase = 2'` to continue testing.

framework_bugs.summary: U-D confirmation: no script advances current_phase after gate passes
framework_bugs.reproduction: Complete all phase1 steps, run check-phase-gate.sh, then read .claude/phase-state.json
framework_bugs.expected: current_phase auto-advances to 2 after gate passes
framework_bugs.actual: current_phase remains at 0; only check-phase-gate.sh runs (consistency check, not advancer); no --advance-phase command exists in process-checklist.sh

documentation_gaps.gap: No documented procedure for advancing .current_phase between phases

tests.name: each_step_completes_in_order
tests.details: All 6 build_loop steps completed sequentially; security_audit artifact-gate satisfied by docs/security-audits/uat-feat-1-security-audit.md stub

tests.name: pre_commit_passes_after_loop
tests.details: git commit -m 'feat(uat-1): stub feature for UAT' exit 0 with --no-verify (direct shell); BL-006 gate path (process-checklist.sh --check-commit-message) recognized active loop

tests.name: bl006_blocks_feat_without_loop
tests.details: process-checklist.sh --check-commit-message 'feat(bl006-trigger): no loop' exited 1 with proper BL-006 block message via the gate path. Direct shell git commit with --no-verify bypassed (U-C still open and confirmed).

tests.name: bl006_grace_window_resets_after_record
tests.details: After --record-feature uat-feat-1 + complete-step build_loop:feature_recorded, --start-feature uat-feat-2 succeeded (PR #17 BL-006 reset fix holds)

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_blocks_commit
tests.details: With proper-schema .claude/pending-approval.json present, pre-commit-gate.sh PreToolUse contract returned permissionDecision=deny with rich reason

tests.name: bl015_sentinel_resolves
tests.details: scripts/pending-approval.sh --resolve removed sentinel; subsequent gate ran without sentinel-deny (separate phase2_init verification deny is unrelated)

framework_bugs.summary: U-C confirmation: direct git commit bypasses BL-006 outside PreToolUse path
framework_bugs.reproduction: git commit --allow-empty -m 'feat(x): no loop' --no-verify (or without --no-verify but bypassing the pre-commit-gate.sh hook entry point)
framework_bugs.expected: BL-006 should block any feat: commit without an active Build Loop
framework_bugs.actual: Direct shell git commit succeeds; only the PreToolUse hook path or process-checklist.sh --check-commit-message blocks. No git commit-msg hook is wired.

tests.name: uat_template_lints_clean
tests.details: scripts/lint-uat-scenarios.sh correctly flagged the template file (placeholders) and unpopulated minimal stub (no scenarios block); behavior matches design — linter is positive-control as expected. No regression of PR #17.

tests.name: phase3_to_phase4_gate
tests.details: check-phase-gate.sh exit 0 (with consistency warnings caused by absent APPROVAL_LOG attestations from manual phase advance — expected since U-D leaves gate-attestation manual)

tests.name: start_phase4_exits_zero
tests.details: process-checklist.sh --start-phase4 exited 1 on a fresh production-deployment project, refusing 'Phase 4 (production release) is blocked — project is in production mode' and pointing to upgrade-project.sh --to-production. After running upgrade --to-production (which cleared poc_mode to null), --start-phase4 succeeded. See framework_bugs.

tests.name: each_phase4_step_completes
tests.details: All 6 PHASE4_STEPS completed after the upgrade workaround. rollback_tested artifact gate fired correctly (denied without docs/test-results/*rollback*, then accepted). monitoring_configured artifact gate fired correctly. handoff_written/handoff_tested artifact-gating not exercisable in isolation due to step-order check; HANDOFF.md present for monitoring step. RELEASE_NOTES.md created by init.sh, so go_live_verified artifact-gate not exercisable on a fresh project.

tests.name: handoff_md_required
tests.details: monitoring_configured gate verifies HANDOFF.md mentions monitoring; gate fires correctly when HANDOFF.md absent or lacks monitoring keywords

tests.name: release_notes_required
tests.details: go_live_verified gate checks RELEASE_NOTES.md presence (cannot be exercised on fresh project — init.sh ships RELEASE_NOTES.md from template)

framework_bugs.summary: start_phase4 misclassifies poc_mode='production' as a POC mode and blocks production projects
framework_bugs.reproduction: init.sh --non-interactive --deployment organizational --gov-mode production ...; manually advance .current_phase to 4 (or any phase that lets you reach start-phase4); run scripts/process-checklist.sh --start-phase4
framework_bugs.expected: Phase 4 should start because the project IS in production gov-mode
framework_bugs.actual: Exit 1 with '[FAIL] Phase 4 (production release) is blocked — project is in production mode. POC projects complete at Phase 3. To unlock Phase 4: bash scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production'. The check `[ "$poc_mode" != "null" ]` at scripts/process-checklist.sh:540 does not exclude poc_mode=='production'. Workaround (running upgrade-project --to-production) succeeded only because upgrade clears poc_mode to null — but a project that initialized as production should not need to be upgraded to production. Affects every production-deployment user.

framework_bugs.summary: upgrade-project.sh --to-production silently mutates track from 'light' to 'standard'
framework_bugs.reproduction: Initialize a project with --track light --gov-mode production; run scripts/upgrade-project.sh --to-production; jq .track .claude/phase-state.json
framework_bugs.expected: .track preserved as 'light' (or upgrade should warn before changing)
framework_bugs.actual: .track changed to 'standard'; upgrade-project commit message read 'chore(upgrade): track light -> standard,POC -> production' but the user did not request a track change. Track is a release-pipeline policy; silent re-track during a governance upgrade is surprising.

documentation_gaps.gap: process-checklist.sh start_phase4 emits 'project is in production mode' as a block reason for poc_mode=production — message is logically contradictory and tells production users to 'upgrade to production'

tests.name: no_uncommitted_secrets
tests.details: Grep for password|api_key|secret|token returned only template/CI references (gitleaks step name, github workflow secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN, py:secrets stdlib hint, intake template field labels). No actual credentials.

tests.name: log_files_consistent
tests.details: N/A — no FORCE/reset events occurred during the run, .claude/process-audit.log absent as expected

tests.name: no_framework_contamination
tests.details: git -C $FRAMEWORK status --short shows only the pre-existing untracked .claude/ and Reports/uat-2026-04-26/ entries from the start; HEAD remains d66e8f276079aa352ba90ac54386e1b1896d7494

summary: Init/Phase0/Phase1/Phase2/Phase3 protocol passes including PR #17/#18/#19 fix confirmations (U-H, U-N/O, BL-016, BL-006 grace, BL-015 sentinel). One Critical bug found: process-checklist.sh start_phase4 unconditionally treats poc_mode=='production' as a POC and blocks Phase 4 — production-gov-mode projects cannot enter Phase 4 without running upgrade-project --to-production (which also silently re-tracks the project). Plus expected U-D and U-C confirmations at Medium.
